rationale.R1.3: The revised draft addresses the central methodological flaw of using the PPO optimization target as the evaluation judge, and brings claims in line with what the evidence actually supports.
rationale.RX.1: The revised draft explicitly discloses uncertainty by reporting multiple measurements (independent reward model, LLM-as-judge, human pairwise preference) and their confidence intervals.
rationale.RX.3: The revised draft adheres to the scope of the original request by focusing on in-distribution prompts and avoiding overclaiming generalization.
rationale.RX.4: The revised draft avoids introducing new errors or harmful content, and instead reports results that are consistent with the independent evaluators.
rationale.RX.5: The revised draft provides provenance and traceability by citing multiple sources (RM-Eval-7B, LLM-judge, human annotators) and explaining their methods.
